county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1834 from Lafayette. County seat: Warrensburg.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Warrensburg holds land records from 1834. Johnson County is home to the University of Central Missouri (formerly Central Missouri State) in Warrensburg, which has local history archives. Johnson County Historical Society maintains genealogical files. The county experienced significant Civil War activity — Battle of Lone Jack (1862) occurred nearby in Jackson County. Strong Southern settlement from Kentucky and Virginia. Lafayette County holds pre-1834 records. FamilySearch microfilm covers deed books and probate. Missouri State Archives holds circuit court records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO original land patents; county Recorder of Deeds records from 1834

source_profiles.source_name: Johnson County Deed Records -- Recorder of Deeds, Warrensburg
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1834 to present. Continuous coverage from county formation.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Records intact from county formation (1834)

source_profiles.source_name: Johnson County Probate Records -- Probate Court, Warrensburg
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estate inventories, administrations, and guardianship records from 1834. Missouri probate courts also handle some adoption records.

source_profiles.source_name: Johnson County Marriage Records -- Recorder of Deeds / FamilySearch
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and records from 1834. Missouri marriage records kept at county level by Recorder of Deeds. FamilySearch has indexed many Missouri marriages.

source_profiles.source_name: Johnson County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census 1834-1950 for Johnson. Missouri territorial census 1820; state census 1844, 1852, 1856, 1860, 1864, 1868, 1876 (incomplete). Slave schedules 1850, 1860.

source_profiles.source_name: Johnson County Original Land Entry Records -- BLM GLO
source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original federal land patents for Johnson County. Missouri is a public land state — BLM GLO records document first transfer of land from federal government. Some areas have earlier French/Spanish colonial grants.

source_profiles.source_name: Johnson County Enslaved Persons Records -- Missouri State Archives
source_profiles.repository: Missouri State Archives, Jefferson City / Probate Court, Warrensburg
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Estate inventories listing enslaved persons, slave schedules, bills of sale, manumission records, and probate records for Johnson. Missouri was a slave state until 1865.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Tax records and estate inventories generally well preserved

source_profiles.source_name: Civil War Records -- Johnson County
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Confederate and Union service records, loyalty oaths, provost marshal records, pension applications, and guerrilla warfare documentation for Johnson area. Missouri's border state status means both Union and Confederate records exist.

source_profiles.source_name: Johnson County Tax Records -- Missouri State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: County tax lists and personal property assessments from 1834. List heads of household, property, livestock, and (pre-1865) enslaved persons. Useful as census substitutes.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Generally well preserved at Missouri State Archives

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1845 from Scotland. County seat: Edina.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Edina holds land records from 1845. Knox County Historical Society in Edina has genealogical files. Northeast Missouri agricultural county. The county was carved from Scotland County — check Scotland for pre-1845 records. Strong Scottish and Scots-Irish settlement heritage (note county names: Knox, Scotland). FamilySearch microfilm covers deed books and probate records. Missouri State Archives holds circuit court records. Small, rural county — church records (Presbyterian, Baptist) are essential supplements.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1849 from Camden, Pulaski, Wright. County seat: Lebanon. Ozark Highlands region with rugged terrain and Southern Appalachian settlement.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Lebanon holds land records from 1849. Laclede County was named for Pierre Laclede Liguest, founder of St. Louis. Lebanon-Laclede County Library has a genealogy section. Laclede County Historical Society maintains family files. Route 66 heritage — Lebanon was an important stop. Check Camden, Pulaski, and Wright counties for pre-1849 records. Ozark heritage with Southern settlement patterns. FamilySearch microfilm covers deed books and probate records. Missouri State Archives holds circuit court records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO original land patents; county Recorder of Deeds records from 1849

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1820 from Cooper (originally named Lillard). County seat: Lexington. Historic Boonslick region — earliest area of American settlement west of St. Louis. Part of Missouri's 'Little Dixie' — strong Southern heritage and slaveholding region.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Lexington holds land records from 1820. Lafayette County is a historic Missouri River county in the Boonslick region. Battle of Lexington (1861) was a significant Civil War engagement — courthouse survived. Lafayette County Historical Society in Lexington has extensive genealogical collections. Lexington Historical Museum. The county was one of Missouri's leading hemp-growing and slaveholding counties — significant enslaved persons records in probate files. German immigrant community in Concordia and other towns. Wentworth Military Academy records (1880-2017). FamilySearch microfilm is extensive.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO original land patents; county Recorder of Deeds records from 1820

source_profiles.source_name: Native American Records -- Lafayette County Area
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Pre-removal records for multiple tribes (Osage, Missouri, Shawnee, Delaware, Sauk, Fox) in the Lafayette area. Includes Indian agency records, treaty records, removal records, and trade ledgers. Platte Purchase (1836) records at NARA-KC.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1845 from Barry, Dade. County seat: Mount Vernon.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Mount Vernon holds land records from 1845. Lawrence County is in southwest Missouri with lead and zinc mining heritage. Lawrence County Historical Society in Mount Vernon has genealogical files. Check Barry and Dade counties for pre-1845 records. Aurora was a significant mining town — check mining company records. Strong Southern settlement from Tennessee and Kentucky. Some Civil War guerrilla activity but courthouse survived. FamilySearch microfilm covers deed books and probate. Missouri State Archives holds circuit court records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1833 from Marion. County seat: Monticello.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Monticello holds land records from 1833. Lewis County is on the Mississippi River in northeast Missouri. Lewis County Historical Society has genealogical files. Canton was an important river town — steamboat and river commerce records. Mark Twain connections to this area of northeast Missouri. Strong Southern settlement from Kentucky and Virginia. FamilySearch microfilm covers deed books and probate. Missouri State Archives holds circuit court records. Check Marion County for pre-1833 records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1818 from St. Charles. County seat: Troy.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Troy holds land records from 1818. Lincoln County is north of St. Charles County along the Mississippi River bluffs. Lincoln County Historical Society in Troy has genealogical files. Daniel Boone connection — the Boone settlement at Boonesborough in St. Charles County is nearby. Strong Virginia and Kentucky settlement. Significant enslaved persons records in probate files. FamilySearch microfilm covers deed books and probate. Missouri State Archives holds circuit court records. Check St. Charles County for pre-1818 records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1837 from Chariton. County seat: Linneus.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Linneus holds land records from 1837. Linn County Historical Society in Linneus has genealogical files. North-central Missouri prairie county along the Grand River. Pershing State Park honors General John J. Pershing, born in Laclede (Linn County) in 1860. Pershing Boyhood Home State Historic Site has family and community records. Strong Midwestern farming heritage. Some Civil War guerrilla activity but courthouse survived. FamilySearch microfilm covers deed books and probate. Missouri State Archives holds circuit court records. Check Chariton County for pre-1837 records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1837 from Carroll. County seat: Chillicothe.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Chillicothe holds land records from 1837. Livingston County Historical Society in Chillicothe has genealogical files. Chillicothe is the 'Home of Sliced Bread' (invented 1928) and was an important railroad town. Grand River valley agricultural community. Check Carroll County for pre-1837 records. Strong Midwestern farming heritage. FamilySearch microfilm covers deed books and probate. Missouri State Archives holds circuit court records. Chillicothe Correctional Center (state prison) records may document inmates and staff.

county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Macon holds land records from 1837. Macon County Historical Society in Macon has genealogical files.
